title: Notopleura pithecobia

Notopleura pithecobia belongs to the genus Notopleura, which is a group of plants found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The family Rubiaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, and Notopleura pithecobia falls under the subfamily Cinchonoideae. The classification of Notopleura pithecobia is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic analysis.

intro: Notopleura pithecobia is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Notopleura pithecobia include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, such as notopleurine and ursolic acid, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of an extract or tincture, to treat respiratory and infectious diseases.
